roles.citation: Manual Section 1.1.5: 'Events are created by event Producers.' The Power OK event is sent by the node when it detects its power status is within acceptable range.
roles.notes: Direct eventid under Node Power Monitor segment; node sends this event to indicate power is good

roles.citation: Manual Section 1.1.5: 'Events are created by event Producers.' The Power Not OK event is sent by the node when its power status is outside acceptable range.
roles.notes: Direct eventid under Node Power Monitor segment; node sends this event to indicate power failure

roles.citation: Manual Section 5.3.4 'Commands': 'Commands are consumer events that are sent to the line. A command can directly control the line, for example by turning it on or off.' CDI description: '(C) When this event occurs'
roles.notes: First Event group under each replicated Line (6 replications per line, 16 lines total = 96 consumer event slots)

roles.citation: Manual Section 5.3.5 'Indications': 'Indications are producer events that are sent to the bus. Each line has 6 producer events associated with it.' CDI description: '(P) this event will be sent'
roles.notes: Second Event group under each replicated Line (6 replications per line, 16 lines total = 96 producer event slots)

roles.citation: Manual Section 6.2 'Variables': 'Each variable has two events (consumers) associated with it. The first event enables the variable, (true)' CDI description: '(C) Event to set variable #1 true.'
roles.notes: Consumer event to set Variable #1 to true state (32 replications of Logic group)

roles.citation: Manual Section 6.2 'Variables': 'Each variable has two events (consumers) associated with it. The first event enables the variable, (true) and the second disables it. (false)' CDI description: '(C) Event to set variable #1 false.'
roles.notes: Consumer event to set Variable #1 to false state (32 replications of Logic group)

roles.citation: Manual Section 6.2 'Variables': 'Each variable has two events (consumers) associated with it. The first event enables the variable, (true) and the second disables it. (false)' CDI description: '(C) Event to set variable #2 true.'
roles.notes: Consumer event to set Variable #2 to true state (32 replications of Logic group)

roles.citation: Manual Section 6.2 'Variables': 'Each variable has two events (consumers) associated with it. The first event enables the variable, (true) and the second disables it. (false)' CDI description: '(C) Event to set variable #2 false.'
roles.notes: Consumer event to set Variable #2 to false state (32 replications of Logic group)

roles.citation: Manual Section 6.5 'Producers': 'Any logic conditional may generate up to four events when it is true. Each of these four events may be; ignored with none, sent Immediately, or sent After delay.' CDI description: '(P) this event will be sent'
roles.notes: Action Event eventid under each Action group (4 replications per Logic, 32 Logic groups = 128 producer event slots)

roles.citation: Manual Section 7.2 'Linking Virtual Code Lines': 'The EventID for the TX code set will always come from the transmitting node and be entered into the receiving node' This is a consumer link address for receiving track circuit data. CDI description: '(C) Remote Mast Link Address.'
roles.notes: Link Address eventid under each Circuit in Track Receiver (8 replications for Track Circuits 1-8)

roles.citation: Manual Section 7.2 'Linking Virtual Code Lines': 'The EventID for the TX code set will always come from the transmitting node and be entered into the receiving node' This is a producer link address for transmitting track circuit data. CDI description: '(P) Track Circuit Link Address. Copy and Paste into linked Track Circuit. (Read Only)'
roles.notes: Link Address eventid under each Circuit in Track Transmitter (8 replications for Track Circuits 1-8); read-only from node user's perspective
